session_hint: the geocoding API rate limit investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing the 429 rate limit errors from the Google Maps Geocoding API?
turns: Three microservices — order-service, delivery-service, and analytics-service — all call the Google Maps Geocoding API. We're seeing 429 OVER_QUERY_LIMIT responses that throttle all three simultaneously.
Checked the Google Cloud Console API key usage dashboard: we're hitting the 3000 QPM cap. But our traffic estimates show each individual service should be well under 1000 QPM on its own.

Found it in our secrets manager: all three services are configured with the same API key — MAPS_KEY_PROD — pulled from a shared environment config. Their requests are pooled under a single per-key quota, so the combined load trips the limit.
Provisioned separate API keys for each service. 429s dropped to zero. Root cause: all three services sharing a single API key, causing combined request rate to exceed the per-key quota.
